nodes.label: Cost-Aware Swarm Moderation
nodes.rationale: Limits agent engagement to a maximum of two specialist agents per turn, defaulting to one or a direct response to minimize token costs and elapsed time. Activates independent reviews and adversarial cross-checks only when high-risk operations (e.g. production migrations, security audits) are detected.

nodes.label: Factuality Citation Contract
nodes.rationale: Enforces strict facts-only communication by requiring every agent response to contain inline citations mapped to a verification table containing specific sources and evidence. If validation fails, Quorum automatically triggers a correction prompt or withholds the response to prevent hallucination.

nodes.label: Mermaid Diagram Rendering Safety
nodes.rationale: Mermaid diagrams are rendered within React UI but can crash the application if unquoted labels contain special characters, spaces, or HTML. Sanitizing the code by quoting these labels prevents runtime parsing errors.

nodes.label: Hybrid Session Grouping
nodes.rationale: Groups similar workspace sessions into folders using DistilBERT embeddings and cosine similarity. It falls back to lexical Jaccard token overlap for offline resilience and keyword-based explanations.

nodes.label: Local Embeddings API
nodes.rationale: Provides vector embeddings for text chunks using window.system.getEmbeddings, enabling semantic topic clustering and session grouping features without cloud dependencies.

nodes.label: Savant Quorum Application Icon
nodes.rationale: Visual metaphor for multi-agent alignment and consensus within a structured hexagonal network, featuring crosshair alignment for target precision and nodes collaborating around a central orchestrator.

nodes.label: Quorum System Tray Icon (Template)
nodes.rationale: MacOS template image representing the application logo in the system tray, supporting automatic color inversion between light and dark modes.

nodes.label: Consensus Quorum Visual Representation
nodes.rationale: The three-node network graphic in the tray icon visually represents the three-agent consensus quorum architecture.
